setting.note: A century of one Wapishana family plays out across the Rupununi, the vast grass plain of southern Guyana between the Kanuku Mountains and the Brazilian border. Ranches and a Jesuit mission sit among the savannah settlements around Lethem, while a modern thread carries a son of the family north to the coastal capital, Georgetown, some four hundred kilometres away.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Pauline Melville, first published 1997. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: The Ventriloquist's Tale, Pauline Melville (1997). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The green of the Rupununi in the wet season, when the savannah floods and the grass runs to the foot of the Kanukus. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
